description: Immerse yourself in the enchanting atmosphere of Vienna, a city where history, art, and culture intertwine beautifully. Over the course of seven days, you and your family will explore stunning palaces, vibrant markets, and serene parks, all while indulging in the local cuisine and shopping for unique souvenirs. This leisurely-paced journey allows you to soak in the city's rich heritage and modern charm, making it the perfect getaway for culture enthusiasts and nature lovers alike.

days.title: Arrival in Vienna and City Exploration

activities.description: Start your day at the iconic Café Central, a historic coffee house known for its elegant atmosphere and delicious pastries. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh bread, butter, and jam, paired with a rich cup of coffee.
Explore the magnificent Schönbrunn Palace, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Stroll through the beautifully landscaped gardens and take a guided tour of the opulent rooms where the Habsburgs once lived.
Head to Naschmarkt, Vienna's famous market, for lunch. Sample local delicacies and international cuisine from various stalls. Don't miss trying a traditional schnitzel or some fresh produce.
After lunch, take a leisurely stroll along Mariahilfer Straße, Vienna's main shopping street. Browse through shops, boutiques, and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere of this bustling area.
Spend your afternoon at the Museum Quarter, home to several renowned museums including the Leopold Museum and the Museum of Modern Art. Immerse yourself in Vienna's rich art scene.
Enjoy a traditional Viennese dinner at Plachutta, famous for its Tafelspitz (boiled beef). Experience authentic Austrian cuisine in a cozy setting, perfect for families.

activities.activity: Evening walk along the Danube Canal
activities.description: End your day with a relaxing walk along the Danube Canal. Enjoy the beautiful views of the city at night, and perhaps stop for a drink at one of the riverside bars.

days.title: Art and History in the Heart of Vienna

activities.description: Start your day at the historic Café Central, a favorite among famous intellectuals. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh pastries and coffee in a stunning setting.
Explore the magnificent Belvedere Palace, home to an impressive collection of Austrian art, including Gustav Klimt's 'The Kiss'. The gardens offer a beautiful view of the city.
Enjoy a hearty lunch at Gasthaus Pöschl, known for its traditional Viennese dishes like Wiener Schnitzel. The cozy atmosphere makes it a perfect spot for families.
Immerse yourself in art at the Kunsthistorisches Museum, one of the world's most important art museums. Don't miss the Egyptian and Near Eastern Collection.
Take a leisurely walk along the Ringstraße, admiring the stunning architecture of the Vienna State Opera and the Parliament building. It's a great way to soak in the city's history.
Indulge in a traditional Tafelspitz dinner at Plachutta Wollzeile, famous for its boiled beef. The restaurant's warm ambiance is perfect for family dining.
Start your day at the historic Café Central, a favorite among intellectuals and artists. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh pastries and coffee in a stunning setting.
Explore the iconic St. Stephen's Cathedral, a masterpiece of Gothic architecture. Climb the tower for breathtaking views of Vienna and admire the intricate details of the interior.
Savor traditional Austrian cuisine at Gasthaus Pöschl, known for its cozy atmosphere and delicious Wiener Schnitzel. A perfect spot to refuel before more sightseeing.
Take a leisurely walk down Graben Street, one of Vienna's most famous shopping streets. Enjoy window shopping at luxury boutiques and local shops, soaking in the vibrant atmosphere.
Discover the grandeur of the Vienna State Opera. If time allows, consider taking a guided tour to learn about its rich history and stunning architecture.
Indulge in a slice of Sachertorte or another delicious pastry at Demel, a historic pastry shop. Enjoy the elegant ambiance while sipping on a cup of coffee.
End your day with a hearty dinner at Plachutta, famous for its Tafelspitz (boiled beef). Experience authentic Viennese dining in a warm and welcoming environment.

Start your day with a delightful breakfast at the historic Café Central, known for its elegant atmosphere and delicious pastries. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh bread, cheeses, and coffee.

Stroll through the expansive Prater Park, where you can enjoy the lush greenery and the iconic Giant Ferris Wheel. It's a perfect spot for a leisurely walk or a bike ride, soaking in the natural beauty of Vienna.
Indulge in a hearty lunch at Schweizerhaus, famous for its traditional Austrian dishes, especially the pork knuckle and fresh beer. The outdoor seating offers a lovely view of the park.

Take a short walk or bike ride to Danube Island, a beautiful recreational area perfect for relaxation. Enjoy the scenic views of the Danube River and perhaps take a dip in the water if the weather permits.
Head to Lainzer Tiergarten, a stunning nature reserve on the outskirts of Vienna. Explore the walking trails and enjoy the tranquility of the forest, where you might spot some wildlife.
End your day with a cozy dinner at Gasthaus Pöschl, known for its authentic Austrian cuisine. Try the Wiener Schnitzel, a local favorite, in a warm and inviting atmosphere.

activities.activity: Evening Walk along the Danube Canal
activities.description: Take a leisurely evening stroll along the Danube Canal, where you can enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and perhaps stop for a drink at one of the many bars lining the water.

days.title: Day Trip to the Wachau Valley

transit.memo: Take a scenic train ride along the Danube River to Dürnstein.

activities.description: Stroll through the charming streets of Dürnstein, famous for its medieval castle ruins and stunning views of the Danube. Don't miss the beautiful blue church tower that dominates the skyline.
Hike up to the ruins of Dürnstein Castle, where Richard the Lionheart was imprisoned. The panoramic views of the Wachau Valley from the top are breathtaking and well worth the effort.
Enjoy a traditional Austrian lunch at a local heuriger (wine tavern) in Dürnstein. Sample local wines and hearty dishes like schnitzel or goulash in a cozy atmosphere.
Take a short train ride from Dürnstein to Melk, where you will visit the magnificent Melk Abbey, a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its stunning baroque architecture and beautiful gardens.
Explore the opulent interiors of Melk Abbey, including the grand library and the church with its impressive frescoes. The views from the abbey over the Danube are simply spectacular.
Take some time to relax by the Danube River, enjoying the serene views and perhaps a leisurely walk along the riverbank. It's a perfect way to unwind after a day of exploration.
Savor a delightful dinner at a local restaurant in Melk, where you can try regional specialties and enjoy the warm hospitality of the area.
Head back to Vienna by train, reflecting on a day filled with history, culture, and stunning landscapes along the Danube.

Start your day at the iconic Café Central, a historic coffee house known for its elegant atmosphere and delicious pastries. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh bread, butter, and jam, paired with a rich cup of coffee.

Explore the whimsical Hundertwasserhaus, an apartment building designed by the artist Friedensreich Hundertwasser. Its colorful façade and unique architectural style make it a must-see for art lovers and photographers alike.

Head to Naschmarkt, Vienna's famous market, where you can sample a variety of local and international foods. Enjoy a casual lunch at one of the many stalls or small restaurants, offering everything from fresh produce to delicious street food.

Wander through the charming Spittelberg district, known for its narrow cobblestone streets and historic buildings. This area is filled with quaint shops, galleries, and cafés, perfect for a leisurely afternoon stroll.

Discover modern Austrian art at the Leopold Museum, home to an impressive collection of works by Egon Schiele and Gustav Klimt. The museum's contemporary design contrasts beautifully with the historic architecture of the surrounding area.
Take a break at Café Sperl, another classic Viennese coffee house. Enjoy a slice of Sachertorte or Apfelstrudel while soaking in the traditional ambiance and perhaps playing a game of chess.
End your day with a hearty dinner at Gasthaus Pöschl, known for its authentic Austrian cuisine. Try the Wiener Schnitzel or Tafelspitz, and enjoy the cozy atmosphere of this local favorite.

transit.memo: Direct flight back to Washington D.C.

activities.description: Start your day at the historic Café Central, a favorite among locals and tourists alike. Enjoy a traditional Viennese breakfast with fresh pastries and coffee in a stunning setting that has hosted famous intellectuals.
Explore the magnificent Schönbrunn Palace, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Stroll through the beautiful gardens and take a guided tour of the opulent rooms that once housed the Habsburgs.
Enjoy a hearty lunch at Gasthaus Pöschl, known for its authentic Austrian cuisine. Try the Wiener Schnitzel, a local favorite, in a cozy atmosphere.
After lunch, take a leisurely walk through the beautiful Belvedere Gardens. The gardens offer stunning views of the city and are a perfect spot for some relaxation before your flight.
Make your way to the airport for your flight back to Washington D.C. Ensure you arrive at least 2 hours before your departure time for a smooth check-in process.
